title: DENV Titration
author: Fabio Gomes
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zsdf6a6

steps.instruction: Dump Methylcellulose overlay and blot on paper towels
Wash gently with 1X PBS. Incubate for 10 min. Blot and dry. 00:10:00
Add 0.5 mL fixative to each well. Allow plates to sit at room temperature for 10 min. Note: Fixed cells can be stored at -70oC for future use. Leave methanol on cells when freezing.
Wash plates with PBS. Incubate for 10 min. 00:10:00 If frozen plates are being used, incubate at 37oC for 30 min.
Prepare antibody dilutions in 5% skim milk, PBS 5 Mass / % volume, Skim Milk 4G2 1:2000
Add 200 µl to each well. Incubate at 37oC for 1 hour on a rocker. 01:00:00 37 °C Minimize contact with each well by maintaining contact of the pipette tip with the wall of well.
Dump off primary antibody solution and tap plates on paper towels to remove excess solution
Wash with 1 mL 5% skim milk, PBS
Prepare antibody dilutions in 5% skim milk, PBS. Anti-mouse-Per 1:2,000
Add 200 µl to each well. Incubate at 37oC for 1 hour on a rocker. 01:00:00 37 °C
Dump off antibody solution. Tap on paper towels
Wash twice with PBS. Tap on paper towel
Add 160 µl of TrueBlue substrate per well
Place on rocker at RT until plaques develop (10 min or longer).
Count plates or incubate at 4oC for up to 7 days.
